_meta.description: Cadence profiles for answer composition. These patterns keep prose from flattening into same-length, same-entry assistant language.

runtimeUsageNotes.consumer: style decision resolver and compose style contract prompt
runtimeUsageNotes.intent: Give the composer a small set of repeatable cadence plans. Rhythm should feel controlled and natural, not stylized for its own sake.

patterns.purpose: Useful for executive and finance answers.
patterns.effect: Puts the conclusion early, then backs it with one fuller sentence.

patterns.purpose: Good default when the answer needs a little explanation and then a clean landing.
patterns.effect: Lets the answer breathe without becoming rambly.

patterns.purpose: Useful when the answer turns on a condition, exception, or distinction.
patterns.effect: Keeps nuance tight instead of scattering caveats everywhere.

patterns.purpose: Analyst cadence for a dense support sentence followed by a simpler synthesis.
patterns.effect: Allows precision without trapping the whole paragraph in analyst prose.

patterns.purpose: Sensitive cadence that stays measured without sounding clinical.
patterns.effect: Keeps emotional temperature low and control high.

patterns.purpose: Compare cadence that alternates delta and consequence.
patterns.effect: Prevents compare answers from turning into side-by-side document summaries.

patterns.purpose: Operational cadence for status, blocker, next step.
patterns.effect: Keeps work-product answers directional and usable.

patterns.purpose: Finish a paragraph with a decisive line after explanation.

rules.trigger: Three same-length sentences in a row
Too many sentences start the same way
List-heavy answer stays list-heavy to the end
